title: Meals on Wheels South Africa Announces 2025 Extreme Cycle for Hunger Tour
description: GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - Meals on Wheels...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - Meals on Wheels South Africa, a leading non-profit organisation dedicated to alleviating hunger and poverty, is thrilled to announce the upcoming 2025 Extreme Cycle for Hunger.


> This annual cycling event brings together passionate individuals who share a commitment to making a difference in the lives of vulnerable South Africans.


The Extreme Cycle for Hunger is a multi-day cycling tour that traverses the breathtaking South African landscape, raising crucial funds and awareness for Meals on Wheels' vital programmes.

This year's event will take place from 25 February to 7 March, with cyclists embarking on a challenging yet rewarding journey across four provinces.

"We are incredibly excited to embark on another inspiring Extreme Cycle for Hunger," says Gershon Naidoo, National Programmes and Marketing Director.

"This event is not only a physical challenge but also a testament to the power of collective action. Through the dedication of our cyclists and the generous support of our partners, we can make a significant impact in combating hunger and poverty in South Africa."

Meals on Wheels South Africa is proud to partner with JAC Motors South Africa, a leading automotive company, as the official vehicle sponsor of the Extreme Cycle for Hunger.

JAC Motors South Africa's support will play a crucial role in ensuring the safe and efficient transportation of cyclists, support staff, and essential equipment throughout the event.


> "We are honoured to partner with Meals on Wheels South Africa for the Extreme Cycle for Hunger," says Karl-Heinz Göbel, CEO of JAC Motors South Africa. "This event aligns perfectly with our commitment to social responsibility and community upliftment. We believe in the power of cycling to inspire and unite, and we are proud to support this worthy cause."


The Extreme Cycle for Hunger is open to cyclists of all levels, from seasoned professionals to enthusiastic amateurs.

Participants will have the opportunity to experience the beauty of South Africa while contributing to a meaningful cause.

To register for the event or to learn more about sponsorship opportunities, please visit www.mowcsnc.org.za.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
